Date 2/05/25 9.10 Packet Pg. 62 AGREEMENT BETWEEN TOWN OF RIVERHEAD ON BEHALF OF TOWN OF RIVERHEAD POLICE ATHLETIC LEAGUE AND NASSAU COUNTY POLICE ACTIVITY LEAGUE – COUNTY LACROSSE UNIT THIS AGREEMENT made and entered into this day of , 2025, by and between the Town of Riverhead, a municipal corporation duly existing under the laws of the State of New York, (hereinafter referred to as the "Town”), with its principal place of business located at 4 West Second Street, Riverhead, NY and Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit, 167 South Broadway, Hicksville, NY 11801. WHEREAS, the Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League, a functional endeavor administered within the Riverhead Town Police Department on behalf of the Town of Riverhead that provides organized sports programs, activities, and education for children ages 5 -13 designed with the intent of giving children healthy social and behavioral skills and the goal being to reduce their susceptibility to gang involvement, alcohol and drug use and delinquency; and WHEREAS, the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit provides professional officials to referee games; and WHEREAS, the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit has offered to provide professional officials to referee games for the 2025 Boy’s Lacrosse program offered to boys Grades K-8 in Riverhead that are part of and affiliated with the Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League; and WHEREAS, by Resolution # 2025- adopted on February 19 , 2025, the Town Board authorized the Supervisor to enter into an agreement with Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit for referee services for 2025 Boys Lacrosse program as part of and affiliated with the Town of R iverhead Police Athletic League in an amount not to exceed $3,060.00 ($2,240.00 Referee fee plus $820.00 team fee). NOW THEREFORE , in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein, the parties agree as follows: SECTION 1. Purpose: The purpose of this Agreement is to contract with Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit to provide and arrange for officiating services for the boys’ lacrosse program as part of and affiliated with the Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League to increase the public health, safety, and quality of life for the youth of the Town of Riverhead. The extent of the Town's payment for services hereunder to Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit is intended to provide social and recreational programs and services that enable youth, especially from poor or otherwise disadvantaged family backgrounds, to participate in these healthy and productive programs to reduce crime and increase youth opportunities for productive activities within the community. SECTION 2. Scope of Work: Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit shall provide and arrange for officiating services for the 2025 Boys Lacrosse program offered to the youth of Riverhead as part of and affiliated with the Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League. 9.10.a Packet Pg. 63 Attachment: 2025 PAL - Nassau Lacrosse Referee Agreement (2025-179 : Nassau Cty Police Activity League) 2 SECTION 3. Duration of Contract: The term of this agreement shall commence on February 19, 2025 and expire at the end of the 2025 Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League Lacrosse season. SECTION 4. Consideration: As consideration for the services provided, as specified in Paragraph 2, the Town will reimburse or provide *payment to the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit for a sum of money not to exceed $3,060.00 ($2,240.00 Referee fee plus $820 .00 team fee ) for 2025 Boys Lacrosse Program to provide and arrange for officiating services for said program(s) that are part of and affiliated with the Town of Riverhead Police Athletic League during the contract period. Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit may submit vouchers seeking payment or reimbursement for monies spent on officiating services on a biweekly or monthly basis during the contract period. *To the extent that Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit seeks payment prior to completion of officiating services, Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit shall be required to provide the Town with documentation to substantiate costs related to such services within 60 days. It is agree d and understood that consideration is limited to payment or reimbursement of monies/costs related to officiating services and no additional costs, charges, or fees are permitted under this agreement. SECTION 5. Relationship: The Town and Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit intend that an independent contractual relationship be created by this contract. The Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit is not considered to be an employee of the Town for any purpose and neither the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit nor any employee of the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit shall be entitled to any of the benefits the Town provides for the Town’s employees, including, but no limited t o, health insurance, sick or annual leave, or workers compensation. The Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit specifically represents and st ipulates that the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit is engaged in the business of providing the services set forth in this contract, whether or not for profit, and that the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit is fully registered and legally a uthorized to conduct such business, and pays all necessary taxes and assessments levied against such business. The terms of this agreement are not intended to create any duties, obligations or liabilities to any third parties. Neither party intends this agreement to create any third-party beneficiaries to this agreement. SECTION 6. Suspension, Termination, and Close Out: If the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit fails to comply with the terms and conditions of this contract, the Town may pursue such remedies as are legally available, including, but not limited to, the suspension or termination of this contract. SECTION 7. Changes, Amendment, Modifications: The Town and Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit may, agree from 9.10.a Packet Pg. 64 Attachment: 2025 PAL - Nassau Lacrosse Referee Agreement (2025-179 : Nassau Cty Police Activity League) 3 time to time, to change or modify the Scope of Work to be performed hereunder. Any such change, amendment or modification shall be in writing and signed by all parties. SECTION 8. Assignability: The Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit shall not assign any interest on this contract and shall not transfer any interest on the contract without prior written consent of the Town. SECTION 9. Reports and Information: The Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit at such times as and in such form as the Town may require, shall furnish the Town such periodic reports, including an accounting for the e xpenditures of Town funds, as it may request pertaining to the work of services undertaken pursuant to the contract, the costs and obligations i ncurred or to be incurre d in connection therewith, and any other matters covered by this contract. SECTION 10. Liability and Indemnification: The Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit agrees to hold the Town harmless and shall indemnify and defend all clai ms, demands, or suits of law or equity arising from the negligent or intentionally wrongful actions or omi ssions of the Nassau County Police Activity League – County Lacrosse Unit officials or agents in providing the services called for under this agreement. SECTION 11. Severability: If any portion of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable for any reason, such holding shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any of the remaining portion. Date 2/05/25 9.23 Packet Pg. 99 § 301-283.31. Requirements for Tier 2 battery energy storage systems. Tier 2 battery energy storage systems are permitted through the issuance of a special permit by the Town Board, pursuant to Article LVII, within the Industrial A Calverton Industrial (CI), Industrial C Light Industrial (LI) , Planned Industrial Park, Agricultural Protection, and Residence A-80 Zoning Use Districts and shall be subject to site plan review by the Planning Board pursuant to Article LVI and as follows: Underlines represent additions Strikethrough represents deletions 9.23.a Packet Pg. 100 Attachment: Amends 301-283.31 Requirements for Tier 2 battery energy storage systems_Amends to remove IND A & IND C and include CI & LI TOWN OF RIVERHEAD PUBLIC NOTICE PLEASE TAKE NOTICE, that a public hearing will be held before the Town Board of the Town of Riverhead, at 4 West Second Street , Riverhead, New York at its regular meeting held on March 4, 2025 at 2:00 p.m. to consider a Local Law to Amend Chapter 301 Zoning and Land Development, Part 3 Supplementary Use Regulations, Article LIID Battery Energy Storage Systems, §301-283.31 Requirements for Tier 2 battery energy storage systems. The intent of the proposed legislation is to implement recommendations of the 2024 Comprehensive Plan Update to remove Industrial A and Industrial C and include Calverton Industrial and Light Industrial. A copy of the entire text of the adopted local law and the provisions of the existing law to be amended may be reviewed at the Office of the Town Clerk, 4 W Second Street, Riverhead, New York 11901, between the hours of 8:30 a.m. and 4:30 p.m., Monday through Friday and can be accessed on the Town of Riverhead website at: Town Board Meeting. Date 2/05/25 9.24 Packet Pg. 117 Downloaded from https://ecode360.com/RI0508 on 2025-01-13 § 301-281. Purpose; permitted districts; definitions. A. It is the intention of the Town Board of the Town of Riverhead, as part of its goal to limit dependence on imported fossil energy and decrease greenhouse gas emissions, to permit commercial solar energy production systems in the industrial zoning use districts to minimize impacts to residents and scenic viewsheds important to the community. B. Commercial solar energy production systems shall be allowed with special permit approval by the Town Board in the Light Industrial (LI) Zoning Use District , Industrial A (Ind A) Zoning Use District, Industrial B District (General Industry) Calverton Industrial (CI) and the Planned Industrial Park (PIP) District. C. Commercial solar energy production systems shall be allowed with special permit approval by the Town Board in the Industrial C (Ind C) Light Industrial (LI) Zoning Use District that is located within the zip code boundary of Calverton. D. Definitions. As used in this section, the following terms shall have the meanings indicated: LOT COVERAGE — The lot coverage shall include the total square footage of the perimeter of all of the solar panels, inclusive of all interior spaces between the panels, in addition to driveways and service roads (paved or stone), and all accessory equipment, buildings and structures. Purposes; permitted districts; definitions. Date 2/05/25 9.25 Packet Pg. 135 § 301-274. Permit required; applicability. A. No telecommunications tower or satellite earth station shall hereafter be used, erected, moved, reconstructed, changed, altered or modified to serve as a telecommunications tower or satellite earth station without the issuance of a special use permit(s) by the Town Board in conformity with the requirements of this article. B. Towers shall be permitted by special permit only in the following zoning use districts: APZ, PIP, PRP, RFC, DRC, SC, BC, CRC, Ind A CI, Ind C LI, RLC, and TRC, but shall not be permitted in whole or in part on any property used for residential purposes. C. Satellite earth stations shall by permitted by special permit only in the IND A CI, IND C LI, PIP and PD Zoning use districts, and shall not be permitted in whole or in part on any property used for residential purposes. D. New towers, antennas and satellite earth stations. All new towers, antennas and satellite earth stations in the Town shall be subject to these regulations, except as otherwise provided hereinbelow. E. Exceptions. The requirements set forth in this article shall not be applicable to: (1) Amateur radio operators' antennas and/or towers not exceeding 50 feet in height, which are owned and operated by a federally licensed amateur radio operator and which are located upon property that is the principal place of business or primary residence of the amateur radio operator. (2) Preexisting towers or antennas. (3) Residential satellite earth stations less than or equal to two meters in diameter. W I T N E S S E T H : WHEREAS, Consultant is an expert in the field of civil engineering, land surveying, and land planning, with an emphasis on matters related to the review and consultation on applications for subdivision, site plan and special permit and other matters involving the planning, design and construction of subdivision improvements, site improvements and public improvements, which include but are not limited to paving, drainage, site disturbance, stormwater runoff and sediment control, landscaping, lighting, and related zoning and planning evaluations; and WHEREAS, over the last two (2) decades Consultant has served as the primary consultant engineer for the Town of Riverhead Planning Board and also provided “as needed” consultant work for the Town Board and its departments, be it Community Development Agency, Highway Department or Engineering Department; and WHEREAS, the Town Engineering Department has recently undertaken the bulk of the review of Planning Board applications and the Consultant’s work has become an “as needed” basis; and WHEREAS, Consultant seeks to terminate the existing consultant agreement with the Town of Riverhead Planning Board and such other Agreements with the Town and instead provide civil engineering, land surveying, and land planning consulting services related to applications for subdivision, site plan, special permit, and/or Town capital improvement projects “as needed” and “on-call” and set forth below. NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows: 9.32.a Packet Pg. 181 Attachment: The Raynor Group Agreement (2025-201 : Authorizes the Supervisor to Execute a PSA with the Raynor Group) 1.0 Scope of Services: 1.1 General: During the term of this Agreement, Consultant shall furnish the engineering, surveying and land planning consulting services set forth below as an independent contractor and not as an employee of Town. 1.2 Specific Services: The Consultant shall perform t he following specific services to the Planning Board, Town Board, Community Development Agency, Highway Department and/or Engineering Department on an “as needed” and “on -call” basis, including but not limited to review and consultation on applications for subdivision, site plan and special permit and other matters involving the planning, design and construction of subdivision improvements, site improvements and public improvements, which include but are not limited to paving, drainage, site disturbance, stormwater runoff and sediment control, landscaping, lighting, and related zoning and planning evaluations. Additionally, the Consultant shall perform engineering, surveying and land planning consulting services on an “as needed” and “on -call” basis for specific Town capital improvement projects if and when authorized by the Town Board. 2.0 Conflict of Interest: 2.1 Conflict of Interest: Consultant hereby represents and covenants that neither it nor any of its employees or representatives has or shall have, directly or indirectly, any agreement or arrangement with any official, employee or representative of the Town of Riverhead, which any such official, employee, representative shall receive either directly or indirectly anything of value whether monetary or otherwise as the result of or in connection with any actual or contemplated application before any Department of the Town, contract with the Town for sale of any product or service. Consultant further represents and covenants that neither it nor any of its employees or representatives have offered or shall offer any gratuity to the Town, its officers, employees, agents, or representatives with a view toward obtaining this Agreement or securing favorable treatment with respect thereto. Consultant further represents that it will not engage in any activity which presents a conflict of interest in light of its relationship with Town. 9.32.a Packet Pg. 182 Attachment: The Raynor Group Agreement (2025-201 : Authorizes the Supervisor to Execute a PSA with the Raynor Group) 2.2 Appearance on Other Projects: In recognition of the Consultant’s reduced and limited representation before the Town Planning Board, the Consultant may, pursuant to §113 -4J(2)(B) of the Town Ethics Code, appear and perform consulting engineering, surveying and land planning services for an individual or entity related to any project that seeks to make application before the Town, provided that such private services are not related to a specific project wherein the Town retained the services of the Consultant. The Consultant shall file a written notice with the Planning Board, Town Board or Town Department immediately or as soon as practicable of the services to be rendered to an individual or entity related to any project within the jurisdiction of the Town. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, this exemption shall not apply to a specific Town capital improvement project authorized by the Town Board unless a special waiver is pursuant to §113 -4J(4) of the Town Ethics Code is obtained . 2.3 Disclosure of Potential Conflicts: In addition to any notices required above, t he Consultant shall promptly disclose and file a written notice of any potential conflicts of interest that may arise during the course of the engagement and the Consultant shall recuse themselves from any project where a conflict exists, unless a special waiver is pursuant to §113 -4J(4) of the Town Ethics Code is obtained. 3.0 Compensation: 3.1 Payment Terms: The Town shall compensate the Consultant at the following rate for services rendered, subject to the terms outlined in the Scope of Work : Engineering and Surveying fees for The Raynor Group, P.E. & L.S. PLLC will be charged in accordance with their 2025 Fee Schedule annexed hereto in Appendix A. 3.2 Invoice Submission: The Consultant shall submit invoices to the Town on a [frequency, e.g., monthly] basis, detailing the individual, their position, hours worked, and a description of the services performed and provided. 4.0 Term and Termination: 4.1 Term: This Agreement shall commence on January 1, 2025, and continue until December 31, 2025, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms herein. 9.32.a Packet Pg. 183 Attachment: The Raynor Group Agreement (2025-201 : Authorizes the Supervisor to Execute a PSA with the Raynor Group) 4.2 Termination: This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either party upon 30 days written notice to the other party. Date 2/14/25 9.38 Packet Pg. 220 02.19.2025 ADOPTED TOWN OF RIVERHEAD TB Resolution 2025-208 RATIFIES THE SETTLEMENT OF LEGAL ACTION AGAINST CARLOS RUBIO, JUDITH RUBIO AND REYNA GARCIA Councilwoman Merrifield offered the following resolution, which was seconded by Councilman Rothwell WHEREAS, a legal action was commenced in the name of the Town of Riverhead against Carlos Rubio, Judith Rubio and Reyna Garcia, the owners of the property known and designated as 1434 Roanoke Avenue, Riverhead, New York, in the Supreme Court of the State of New York, County of Suffolk under Index Number 624760/2023, to enjoin the use and occupancy of said property; and WHEREAS, the parties have reached a settlement of that legal action inclusive of all costs, expenses and interest; and WHEREAS, in light of the costs attendant to proceeding to a trial together with the uncertainties of litigation, the Town Attorney’s Office demanded, and received, certain terms of settlement, including civil penalties payable to the Town in the sum of $7,500.00. NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the Town Board of the Town of Riverhead be and hereby accepts and ratifies the settlement reached regarding legal action in the name of the Town of Riverhead against Carlos Rubio, Judith Rubio and Reyna Garcia, the owners of the property known and designated as 1434 Roanoke Avenue, Riverhead, New York, in the Supreme Court of the State of New York, County of Suffolk under Index Number 624760/2023, to enjoin the use and occupancy of said property; and be it further, RESOLVED, that the Town Board ratifies authorization for the Office of the Town Attorney to enter into a stipulation of settlement on behalf of the Town as well as any other documents necessary to effectuate the settlement of the litigation; and it is further; and be it further, RESOLVED, that the Town Clerk is hereby directed to forward a certified copy of this resolution to the Accounting Department and the Office of the Town Attorney ; and be it further, RESOLVED, that all Town Hall Departments may review and obtain a copy of this resolution from the electronic storage device and, if needed, a certified copy of same may be obtained from the Office of the Town Clerk.
